hypothesis: If a model is trained with a cross-modal predictive architecture that jointly encodes crystal graphs, RoboCrystallographer text descriptions, and simulated XRD spectra and uses latent-space cross-modal prediction with VIC regularization and contrastive loss, then it will achieve lower MAE on crystal property prediction tasks (e.g., band gap, shear/bulk modulus, dielectric, perovskite formation energy) and better generalization to an external AFLOW test set than state-of-the-art graph-only baselines (CGCNN, CoGN, ALIGNN) while requiring updating only a small fraction of parameters during finetuning, because aligning complementary modalities yields more informative and robust material representations.
